observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to continue conducting monthly bank reconciliation and to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD No. 1445, which requires regular reconciliation of the cash in bank account balances between the accounting and bank records to ensure that the Municipality’s financial records are accurate and up-to-date. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Budget Officer with a copy of the SWA for unbilled infrastructure projects that were fully or partially completed as of year-end;
The Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the CAFOA based on the SWA forwarded by the Municipal Engineer, and to furnish a copy of the CAFOA to the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEV to record the cost of unbilled projects, based on their actual status of completion. This will ensure that the corresponding Accounts Payable, PPE and Repairs and Maintenance accounts are fairly and accurately presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to ensure proper investigation and resolution of the unreconciled difference between the physical count balance with the Property and Accounting Unit records and, ultimately with the balances presented in the year-end financial statement to establish a PPE balance that is verifiable as to existence, condition, and accuracy. It is also recommended that the Inventory Committee, together with the concerned officials and employees, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to improve the monitoring and inventory-taking of PPEs and facilitate the timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare a Lapsing Schedule that includes all items under per PPE account, detailing the necessary information for the computation of depreciation expense, such as the asset descriptions, acquisition costs, and acquisition dates, for a more accurate computation of depreciation expense; and
Compute the depreciation expense in accordance with IPSAS 17, COA Circular No. 2004-003 and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to ensure accuracy and reliability of the carrying amounts of PPE reported in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all road networks within the municipality.
The Municipal Engineer, in collaboration with the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate, to thoroughly review their existing records and undertake a comprehensive assessment to accurately identify and account for all existing public infrastructures within the Municipality;
The GSO Designate to update the Property Cards for Road Networks based on the result of the assessment of the Municipal Engineer; and
The Municipal Accountant to utilize the information from the assessment to update the Local Road Network Ledger Cards, in order to facilitate compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT/SET Receivable in the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers with the reported balance of RPT/SET Receivable in the Financial Statements to ensure accuracy in the reported balances as at year-end and prevent under-collection or over-collection of RPT and SET from taxpayers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts, and to record the necessary adjusting entries to correct the reported balances as at year-end. It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement appropriate internal control measures, including the regular and timely reconciliation of these accounts, to facilitate the prompt identification and resolution of discrepancies and ensure accurate reporting of balances.

We recommended that the Local Executive Chief require the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the other departments for the submission of prerequisite documents and reports needed in the preparation of the Financial Statements, and immediately submit the SCBAA and NFS. We further recommend that the management faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports within the prescribed period to facilitate the review thereon and avoid further audit action as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Human Resource Management Officer and the employees responsible for the remittance of premium contributions to take immediate and appropriate action to remit the withheld premium contributions to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG Fund and PhilHealth in order to avoid further accrual of penalties and surcharges, and to ensure that employees retain full access to the benefits and services offered by these agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with other officials concerned, to ensure proper project planning, allocate sufficient funds to avoid delays in the implementation of development projects, and maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LSB to submit the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the DepEd CO, thru appropriate channel, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM within the prescribed timelines.

We recommended that the Local Executive Chief, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculturist and MPDC, ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 Municipal Share through the proper project planning and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with Republic Act No. 9184 and review their procurement procedures to ensure full adherence to procurement laws and regulations. Additionally, the BAC must diligently verify all documents submitted by bidders to prevent eligibility issues and procedural lapses that may compromise the fairness and transparency of the procurement process. Strengthening these measures will enhance procurement efficiency, prevent project delays, and ensure that intended beneficiaries receive the full benefits of government-funded projects on time.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and pertinent COA Circulars by ensuring that procurement processes are properly documented, approved in the correct sequence, and carried out within prescribed timelines. Management should capacitate the BAC and other procurement personnel through training and the establishment of internal controls to promote compliance, transparency, and accountability in all stages of the procurement cycle.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended that the management faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports within the prescribed period.
Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and immediately initiate measures to address the problem. Furthermore, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD No. 1445.
1.5 The Municipal Accountant clarified that the delayed submission of necessary reports from other departments of the Municipal Government ultimately caused the year-end financial statements to be submitted late to the Office of the Auditor, missing the prescribed period. Management acknowledges the audit recommendation and has committed to complying with it. Specifically, the Municipal Accountant has pledged to ensure that the year-end financial statements will be submitted before the February 14 deadline of the succeeding year. Additionally, she will coordinate with other departments to ensure the timely submission of necessary reports, giving her ample time to prepare the financial statements.

2.6 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the Financial Statements, thus, provide adequate information to its users in analyzing and understanding the financial statements pursuant to the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 1.
2.7 The Municipal Accountant has expressed a firm commitment to comply with the audit recommendation. Specifically, she has pledged to address the issue by ensuring the complete disclosure of all necessary financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements. This action aligns with the stipulation outlined by IPSAS 1, aiming to achieve both accuracy and fair presentation of the Municipality’s Financial Statements. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant recognizes the importance of providing comprehensive and reliable information to the users of the Financial Statements.

3.5 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to continue conducting monthly bank reconciliation and to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD No. 1445, which requires regular reconciliation of the cash in bank account balances between the accounting and bank records to ensure that the Municipality’s financial records are accurate and up-to-date.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with each other for the immediate reconciliation and resolution of the noted discrepancy of ₱64,955,391.81 so that the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the Municipality’s financial statements would be ensured.
3.6 The Management acknowledged the recommendation and assured that immediate action will be taken. They are actively engaged in investigating the discrepancy and are committed to providing substantiation promptly. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will collaborate closely to prioritize the reconciliation process and effectively address the identified discrepancy.

4.6 We recommended that the management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count earlier to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE, and to submit such on or before the reglementary period.
4.7 The Management acknowledges the audit observation regarding the accuracy and reliability of the reported Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as at December 31, 2023, and assures the audit team that they will comply with the recommendation. Management will ensure that the RPCPPE is submitted within the prescribed period, thereby meeting regulatory requirements and enhancing the accuracy and reliability of the PPE accounts in future financial statements.

5.4 We reiterated the recommendation that Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to thoroughly review his records and undertake comprehensive assessment to accurately identify and account for all existing public infrastructures within the Municipality, and provide the Municipal Accountant the output of his assessment.
b. The Municipal Accountant to utilize the information from the assessment conducted by the Municipal Engineer to complete the Registry of Public Infrastructures and transfer these Public Infrastructures to the books of accounts as Road Networks to comply with the transitory provision in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
5.5 The Management acknowledges the audit recommendation and will take immediate action to address the audit issue. The Inventory Committee, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant will coordinate with each other to accurately identify and account the existing Road Networks within the Municipality by preparing an Inventory Plan, and to transfer these Public Infrastructures to the book of accounts.

a. Constitute an Appraisal Committee to determine the fair values of each kind and each classification of the animal stock for proper valuation, in accordance with IPSAS;

c. Instruct the Accounting Unit to adopt and implement the rules and guidelines in the proper valuation of the Biological Assets of the agency in order to ascertain the correct presentation and reporting of the Biological Assets account in the financial statements; and
d. Assign/designate a Biological Asset Caretaker to prepare and maintain a BAPC in order to establish a sound control over the Biological Assets of the agency so to comply with Section 123 of PD No. 1445 and Section 2.4.1 of the NGICS and minimize the risk of possible loss.
6.16 The Management acknowledged and appreciated the audit finding and recommendations pertaining to the Biological Assets accounts. They are fully committed to comply with the audit recommendations and to adhere to the relevant provisions in the future.

7.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take action to ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund, which significantly contribute to achieving desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries. Additionally, establish a system of regular monitoring and evaluation of ongoing projects to ensure their timely completion and adherence to set targets which can help identify any issues or challenges that may arise during project implementation; thus, corrective action would be undertaken to address them promptly. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer ascertain proper charging of expenses to the 20% DF.
7.7 The Management, in their response to our AOM, acknowledged the concern raised regarding the 11 PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund that were not fully implemented, and the 3 PPAs with expenditures that are not among the allowable expenditures chargeable against the fund. They assure that these initiatives will be given the utmost priority and will be completed in the ensuing year, and to ensure the proper charging of expenditures in this fund.

8.5 We recommended that the Local Executive Chief ensure the optimal utilization of the RA No. 7171 Municipal Share through the proper and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to improve project scheduling through: (1) eliminating project overlap between by-administration projects to avoid inadequacy of laborers; (2) considering the distance of the project site which affects material hauling and labor transport; and (3) proactive monitoring of the project duration to prevent delays in completion and enable the Municipality to take the appropriate action in case slippages occur.
8.6 In response to our AOM, the Management acknowledged the audit finding and recommendation pertaining to the completion of PPAs funded under the RA No. 7171 Municipal Share. Furthermore, the Management recognizes the significance of adhering the timeline for project implementation and is dedicated to ensuring that all necessary measures are taken to achieve the successful and timely completion of these initiatives, as some of the cited projects have already been completed on the first quarter of the year 2024. They understand the anticipation of the tobacco farmers, who are the intended beneficiaries, and are committed to delivering the expected benefits through these projects.

9.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officer to prioritize the immediate recruitment of competent staff for the LDRRMO. This action will not only ensure compliance with statutory obligations but also significantly bolster the Municipality's resilience to potential disasters. Consequently, it will safeguard lives, property, and resources that are invaluable to the community's wellbeing and development.
9.8 The Management clarified that no plantilla was created for the three required staff positions for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning within the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) due to funding limitations; thus, resulted to designation of a personnel to the staff position handling research and planning in concurrent capacities with her existing position and the designation of Job Order employees for the other two staff positions. This situation strongly suggests that these individuals may not be able to effectively fulfill their LDRRMO roles and responsibilities, potentially compromising the office’s overall effectiveness in disaster risk reduction and management. Fortunately, the Management acknowledges the risk of this shortcoming and is committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

10.8 We recommended that the Municipality consider the advantages of engaging in a Framework Agreement facilitated through Competitive Bidding. This approach will ensure efficiency in procurement, minimize delays in acquisition, provide immediate delivery on short notice, and offer fixed or adjusted prices for the duration of the agreement, among other benefits. Furthermore, by exploring the utilization of Framework Agreements in accordance with GPPB Resolution No. 27-2019, the Municipality can enhance transparency, fairness, and value for money in its procurement processes while effectively meeting the needs of its constituents.
10.9 The Management acknowledges the benefits and advantages of engaging in a Framework Agreement as against their usual procurement of meals and snacks through Small Value Procurement. They are fully committed on the audit recommendation and will consider engaging in Framework Agreement in the future.

For the proper management and handling of Inventory, we recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of each category of PPE as of December of every year. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials concerned to cause the immediate settlement of all suspensions or disallowances before they mature to disallowances or become final and executory. For the proper ma-nagement and handling of the PPE, we have recommended that:
The Inventory Com-mittee conduct the required annual phy-sical count of the PPE as of December of every year. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/ physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unser-viceable assets.
